posts.author: jhyuklee (jhlee)
posts.cookedHtml:
1. One item has 1 to 50 columns.
    Here, columns from column 1 to column 10 are left as items.
    Columns 11 to 30 are sub-items with 1,
    How do I move the function expression from column 31 to column 50 to sub-item 2?
    I would appreciate it if you could tell me specifically.

  2. Is there a number of columns allowed per item for the formula or Monday to operate smoothly? Almost unlimited?

thank you

posts.author: BiancaT (Bianca Taylor)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @jhyuklee,

So that I can get a better understanding of your end goal here, would you be happy to share a screenshot of your set up (providing no confidential information is shared)? Am I correct in understanding that you’d like to convert some parent item columns, into columns related to your subitems?

As for your second question, I can investigate this internally however wanted to check - how many columns would you ideally like to include in the formula column?

posts.author: badoorty (David )
posts.cookedHtml: المشكلة فى عدد الأعمدة وكيفية التحقق منها

posts.author: ajay_parikh (Ajay Parikh)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any updates on this question? I have a similar issue.

I have a form with extensive conditional logic and branching (not a Monday.com form) that users submit which creates items in a Monday.com board. The form allows users to submit 1 Project level request which can contain multiple sub-items.

Right now, we get our board populated with 1 item for each form submission. We’d like to be able to use values in a multi-select field to automatically create the relevant sub-items (which I can do using automation); however, I don’t know how to move or copy the values from the parent columns to the appropriate sub-items - we have different parent columns that would map to different sub-items based on the sub-item type.

Certain columns are meant to be at the item level while other remaining columns are meant to be for different sub-items based on values of a specific field.

Is there a way to convert some parent items columns into sub-item columns?
